stats.cpuTrendAria: Host CPU trend over the last few minutes
stats.trafficTrendAria: Box-wide request volume over the last 24 hours

table.noMatch: No apps match the current filter.

confirm.title: Delete {{name}}?
confirm.description: This permanently removes the app, its services, configuration, and data volumes — plus any managed database it owns. This action cannot be undone.

empty.title: No apps yet
empty.description: Connect a repository to deploy your first app, or import an existing vac.app.yaml spec.

budget.heading: Container Budget
budget.overCommitted: Apps have reserved more RAM than the box has — over-committed.
budget.unbudgeted_one: {{count}} app without a RAM limit isn't budgeted.
budget.unbudgeted_other: {{count}} apps without a RAM limit aren't budgeted.

capacity.title: RAM Capacity
capacity.description: Committed cap vs live usage per app. Committed RAM includes stopped apps; apps without a limit aren't budgeted.

new.title: New Application
new.subtitle: Connect a Git repository — VAC handles build, deploy, HTTPS and routing.

source.title: Connect a Repository
source.subtitle: Paste a Git URL. VAC creates a read-only deploy key for private repos.
source.repoHint: HTTPS for public repos, or SSH (git@…) for private repos with a deploy key.
source.repoInvalid: Enter a valid Git URL — HTTPS (https://…) or SSH (git@host:owner/repo).
source.portMissing: No port set — this image won't be reachable over HTTP. Set the container's listening port to expose it; leave empty only for a non-HTTP worker.
source.slugTaken: An app named <0>{{slug}}</0> already exists. Pick a different name.
source.slugInvalid: Use letters, numbers, and hyphens — at least one letter or number.
source.imageSubtitle: Point VAC at a published image. It pulls and runs it — no repo, no build.
source.imageRefHint: e.g. ghcr.io/me/app:1.4.2 or nginx:alpine. A moved tag re-pulls on deploy; a @sha256 digest is pinned.
source.internalPortHint: The port your container listens on, exposed to the VAC edge. Leave empty for a non-HTTP worker.

registry.hint: Add credentials if the image is in a private registry. Stored encrypted; never shown again.
registry.hostHint: e.g. ghcr.io. Leave empty for Docker Hub.

build.title: Build Configuration
build.subtitle: Choose how VAC builds this repo, or let it auto-detect.
build.imageNote: Prebuilt image — no build step. VAC pulls the image and runs it.

domain.title: Domain
domain.subtitle: Custom domains are added after the first deploy — here's how it works.
domain.note: Point a <0>CNAME</0> (or A record) at this VPS, then add the domain from the app's <1>Domains</1> tab after the first deploy — certificates are issued on the first request. Until then, your app is reachable at its generated URL.

env.title: Environment Variables
env.subtitle: Optional. Set what your app needs before its first deploy — load the repo's .env.example, paste a .env, or add them by hand.
env.notFound: No .env.example found in this repository — add variables manually below.

unreadable.title: Couldn't read the repository
unreadable.body: This looks like a private repo. Its deploy key doesn't exist yet, so the .env.example can't be read here. Add variables manually now, or load them from the app's Variables tab once the deploy key is registered.

env.saveFailed: App created, but saving variables failed
env.empty: No variables yet — load, paste, or add them.
env.hint: Secrets (the lock icon) are stored encrypted and never shown again. You can edit these later in the app's Variables tab.
env.pastePrompt: Paste the contents of a .env file. Blank lines and # comments are ignored.

review.title: Review & Deploy
review.subtitle: Create the app, verify access, then run the first deploy — all here.
review.name: Name

sshKey.title: This SSH repo needs a deploy key
sshKey.body: Create the app first — VAC then generates a read-only deploy key. Add it to your Git host and test the connection to unlock the first deploy.

addKey.title: Add this deploy key to your Git host
addKey.body: Copy the key below and add it as a read-only deploy key on your Git host (Deploy keys / SSH keys with read access). Then run the connection test — deploy unlocks once it succeeds.

connect.reach: Confirm VAC can reach <0>{{url}}</0> before the first deploy.
connect.deployLocked: Add the deploy key to your Git host and pass the connection test to unlock deploy.

sourceType.heading: How do you want to deploy?

framework.label: Framework
framework.hint: React, Next.js, Astro, Vite, Node, Python

buildSource.autoNote: VAC inspects the repo at deploy time and picks the first match: a compose file, then a Dockerfile, then a known framework. Override above if you want a specific build.
buildSource.frameworkDetected: Detected a {{name}} app — VAC can build it automatically.
buildSource.buildCommandHint: Runs in the build stage. Leave empty for the default.
buildSource.outputDirHint: Folder containing index.html, relative to the repo root.
buildSource.spaFallbackHint: Serve index.html for unknown paths (client-side routing).

import.title: Import App
import.description: Paste a <0>vac.app.yaml</0> spec (or upload one) to create or update an app. Re-importing the same slug updates it in place. Sensitive env values aren't carried — you re-enter them afterwards.
